project.name: Criminal justice institutions and the social trajectories of adolescents/youth in Burkina Faso: endogenous/local forms and patterns of punishment

titleInfo.title: Synthèse d’entretien avec le kaam naaba
Summary of interview with kaan naaba

This interview looks at the various forms of circulation of knowledge and models of punishment of adolescents/youth in pre- and post-colonial societies. The circulation of these forms of knowledge and models of punishment are often relayed by contemporary legal institutions such as gendarmeries, police forces and courthouses in Burkina Faso.

abstract: Following an interview with the Kaam Naaba of Manga, this report describes the types of misconduct and the procedure for dealing with them.  The report also presents the respondent's perception of the notion of punishment, judgment, condemnation and punishment in relation to gender and social class. Finally, the report describes the collaboration between traditional and modern justice, as well as the popular perception of prison as a model of punishment.

abstract: This summary of an interview with anspecialist education associate, supervised education option, took place in Manga on April 30, 2021. During this interview, the participants discussed the typology of misconduct, as well as its management for both modern justice actors and traditional judicial bodies. The discussion also looked at the perception of punishment as a function of gender and social class, the meaning of mediation, and collaboration between traditional and modern institutions in the management of misconduct.

note: Interview with the Samandin Naaba of Ouagadougou
abstract: This interview summary was conducted on April 25, 2021 with the Samandin Naaba of Ouagadougou. During the discussions, participants exchanged views on the choice of actors designated to deliver customary justice, the model of punishment instituted, and the forms of punishment in relation to gender and social class. Participants also discussed popular perceptions of these forms of punishment, as well as collaboration between the two bodies (customary and modern), and the perception of traditional justice by modern justice.

affl: Catholic University of West Africa/University of Bobo-Dioulasso

abstract: This summary was written following an interview with a Bobo insider in Bobo Dioulasso. These exchanges concerned the types of faults encountered among the Bobo, the actors of Bobo customary justice, as well as the process of crime management among the Bobo. Discussions also focused on the meaning of community mediation, popular perceptions of modern and customary justice, and popular perceptions of prisons as a model of punishment for children.

affl: Tribunal de Grande Instance (TGI) de Manga

note: Interview with Balm Naaba of Manga, representative of the Manga Cathedral Parish and an Imam from Manga
abstract: This text presents a general summary of the data collected in Manga as part of the research project. The actors interviewed were the Kaam Naaba (customary chief), the head of social services at the Manga House of Arrest and Correction (MACM), the children's judge, the Catholic chaplain at the MACM and the Balm Naaba (customary chief). These interviews focused on management types and procedures, types of punishment and their relationship with gender and social class. Community mediation mechanisms and the population's perception of the different types of punishment and judicial bodies (modern and traditional).

abstract: This text presents the general synthesis of data collected in Ouagadougou as part of the research project: Institutions judiciaires pénales et trajectoires sociales des adolescents/jeunes au Burkina Faso: formes et modèles de punitions endogènes/locales. The actors who took part in these interviews were: the examining magistrate, the juvenile judge, the BRPE commissioner and representatives of the customary authorities. These interviews focused on the types and procedures of management, types of punishment and their relationship with gender and social class. Community mediation mechanisms and the population's perception of the different types of punishment and judicial bodies (modern and traditional).

titleInfo.title:
Institutions judiciaires et adolescents au Burkina Faso :
des modèles de punitions endogènes et contemporaines
judical institutions and adolescents in Burkina Faso: endogenous and contemporay models of punishment

This interview looks at the various forms of circulation of knowledge and models of punishment of adolescents/youth in pre- and post-colonial societies. The circulation of these forms of knowledge and models of punishment are often relayed by contemporary legal institutions such as gendarmeries, police forces and courthouses in Burkina Faso.

affl: Centre Africain de recherche pour une pratique culturelle du Développement

abstract:
“This study on ‘patterns of punishment in the Bobo community of Bobo-Dioulasso’ presents in the first part the geographical situation of Bobo communities, their population and their social organization. The second part of the report presents  
the results of interviews conducted with various resource persons, 10 of whom were involved in carrying out this study in the Bobo community in the Bobo-Dioulasso area. These included teacher-researchers, historians, the Catholic University of West Africa, administrative, customary and religious authorities.”

sponsor: EXC2052/1 Africa Multiple, DFG Project number 390713894

project.name: Criminal justice institutions and the social trajectories of adolescents/youth in Burkina Faso: endogenous/local forms and patterns of punishment

titleInfo.title: Étude de recherche les modèles de punitions dans la communauté bobo de Bobo-Dioulasso
Research study on punishment patterns in the Bobo-Dioulasso community

This interview looks at the various forms of circulation of knowledge and models of punishment of adolescents/youth in pre- and post-colonial societies. The circulation of these forms of knowledge and models of punishment are often relayed by contemporary legal institutions such as gendarmeries, police forces and courthouses in Burkina Faso.

affl: Centre africain de recherche pour une pratique culturelle du développement
Commissariat Central de Police de Bobo-Dioulasso
Tribunal de Grande Instance (TGI) de Bobo-Dioulasso

note: Interview with Bishop emeritus of Bobo Dioulasso, Canton Chief Bobo Mandaré. Law enforcement officials and other prominent community leaders.
abstract: Punishment, i.e. subjecting someone to a penalty in expiation for a fault, can be constructive or destructive for the person sentenced. Faced with the current crisis in educational systems, does punishment not aggravate violence? This is the question to which this report seeks to provide some answers. To this end, the author develops his thinking in three points. The first is a practical approach to the intelligibility of present-day reality, i.e. understanding how the behavior of today's teenagers came to be.  The second part seeks to understand the customs and institutions designed and implemented by certain Burkinabe societies over the centuries to correct adolescents. Finally, the third part makes proposals for the education and correction of adolescents, with a view to making them true citizens, aware of their role in building a prosperous nation that contributes to the construction of the universal.

sponsor: EXC2052/1 Africa Multiple, DFG Project number 390713894

project.name: Criminal justice institutions and the social trajectories of adolescents/youth in Burkina Faso: endogenous/local forms and patterns of punishment

titleInfo.title: Réinsertion socio-professionnelle des détenus dans les maisons d’arrêt et de correction du Burkina Faso : cas de la Maison d’Arrêt et de Correction de Bobo-Dioulasso (MACB)
Socio-professional reintegration of inmates in remand prisons in Burkina Faso: the case of the Bobo-Dioulasso Remand Prison (MACB)

This interview looks at the various forms of circulation of knowledge and models of punishment of adolescents/youth in pre- and post-colonial societies. The circulation of these forms of knowledge and models of punishment are often relayed by contemporary legal institutions such as gendarmeries, police forces and courthouses in Burkina Faso.

abstract: According to the International Prison Observatory, prison conditions are generally considered to be poor in every country in the world, although there are notable differences from one country to another. In Burkina Faso, the problem lies in hygiene, snacks and prison overcrowding, which is a major obstacle to the socio-professional integration of inmates, increasing the number of repeat offenders in all the country's prisons. This situation is due to the political, social and economic program governing the prison environment. This raises the question: what are the accompanying measures and the consequences of incarceration conditions on the socio-professional reintegration of prisoners?  To answer this question, the present study was carried out at the Bobo-Dioulasso prison (MACB).
